name: core
description: LambdaCore-shaped utility packages. Catalog code calls into these helpers — `$command_utils:object_match_failed`, etc. — instead of inlining the same notify-and-bail branches per verb. Each helper class mirrors a sibling LambdaCore object (#219 command_utils, #20 string_utils, #3669 object_utils, …); their parent class `$utils` mirrors LambdaCore #288 (Generic Utilities Package). Initial release ports just the verbs we need from $command_utils; sibling packages and additional verbs land as catalog code reaches for them.

classes.description: Marker base for utility singletons — mirrors LambdaCore #288 (Generic Utilities Package). Doesn't carry behavior; subclasses are the actual helper objects ($command_utils, future $string_utils / $object_utils / etc.). Wizard-owned and read-only by convention.

LambdaCore $command_utils (#219). Helpers that command verbs lean on so the same notify-and-bail branches don't get inlined into every verb body. Initial port carries only :object_match_failed; the rest of the LambdaCore surface (`:player_match_failed`, `:do_huh`, `:read`, `:yes_or_no`, `:running_out_of_time`, `:explain_syntax`, …) lands as woo verbs need them.

verbs.name: object_match_failed

classes.description: LambdaCore $string_utils (#20). Initial port carries only :names_of (the renderer that @contents / @kids / @parents lean on); the rest of the LambdaCore string surface (`:trim`, `:english_list`, `:abbreviated_value`, `:left`, `:right`, `:centre`, …) lands as woo verbs need them. woo's substrate already provides `str_trim`, `str_lower`, `str_starts`, `str_index`, `str_slice`, `str_char`, `str_join` as builtins, so the verb-side $string_utils helpers are the LambdaCore conveniences that compose on top.

verbs.name: names_of
to_value

classes.description: LambdaCore $code_utils (#153). Initial port carries :parse_propref / :parse_verbref / :parse_argspec / :toint / :nth_verb_name — the helpers that split <obj>.<prop> and <obj>:<verb> tokens into their parts and translate verb-by-number forms. The rest of LambdaCore $code_utils (find_verb_named, explain_verb_syntax, short_prep, …) lands when a woo verb reaches for it.

verbs.name: parse_propref
parse_verbref
parse_argspec
toint
nth_verb_name
